title: ficus tuphapensis var. annamensis
intro: Ficus tuphapensis var. annamensis, also known as Annam fig, is a species of fig that is native to Vietnam. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Vietnamese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system. Ficus tuphapensis var. annamensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, allowing for various forms of consumption and utilization in traditional medicine.
